# Cyberlounge Portfolio

> Personal portfolio + lead-gen site (cyberlounge.net) for Nicholas Perry's software & cloud consulting, built on Next.js 15 App Router.

## Project Overview

**Tech Stack:** Next.js 15.1.7, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 3, Framer Motion 12, rss-parser
**Status:** Active (deployed to Vercel)
**Purpose:** Showcase projects, surface Medium writing, route prospects to a Cal.com booking flow

## Core Documentation

- `README.md` — Setup and getting started (note: references stale Next.js 14)
- `CODE_MAP.md` — Feature-to-file map
- `docs/ai/memory.md` — Project history and current state
- `docs/ai/roadmap.md` — Forward-looking priorities
- `docs/ai/tasks.md` — Active work + tech-debt list
- `docs/ai/decisions.md` — Architecture decisions (ADRs)
- `docs/ai/architecture.md` — System design
- `ENTRY_POINTS.md` — Where execution starts
- `DATA_FLOW.md` — System-level data movement
- `FEATURE_BOUNDARIES.md` — Component responsibilities
- `IMPORT_GRAPH_SUMMARY.md` — Coupling overview

## Key Files by Area

### Site shell
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — Root layout, metadata, Navbar/Footer mount
- `src/components/Navbar.tsx` — Top nav with route highlighting + mobile menu
- `src/components/Footer.tsx` — Footer (quick links + socials)
- `src/app/globals.css` — Global Tailwind styles, cyberpunk theme

### Pages
- `src/app/page.tsx` — Home
- `src/app/about/page.tsx` — About
- `src/app/projects/page.tsx` — Projects gallery
- `src/app/blog/page.tsx` — Blog (fetches Medium posts client-side)
- `src/app/contact/page.tsx` — Contact (Cal.com + social links)

### API
- `src/app/api/medium-posts/route.ts` — `GET /api/medium-posts`: Medium RSS proxy

### Shared
- `src/components/MotionWrapper.tsx` — Client-boundary `MotionDiv` re-export

## Common Tasks

**To run locally:** `npm install && npm run dev` → http://localhost:3000
**To build:** `npm run build && npm start`
**To lint:** `npm run lint`
**To change which Medium account is shown:** edit the hardcoded URL in `src/app/api/medium-posts/route.ts` (env var path is documented in README but not wired)

## Environment Variables

- `NEXT_PUBLIC_MEDIUM_USERNAME` — Documented in README but not consumed by current code. Update the route handler before relying on it.
